CURRENT KD2 VS CURRENT KD4 CODEX-RS EXHAUSTIVE AUDIT
====================================================

Generated: 2026-07-09

Authoritative inputs
--------------------

KD2:
  C:\Users\kuh\Desktop\codexKD2\codex-rs
  Current commit at final scan:
  9afd23d0be106cfe2520235276fd5c2a9616a19c

KD4:
  C:\Users\kuh\Desktop\kd4\codex-rs
  Current commit at final scan:
  f84519c4d79be55b34eac3a32b90cd787ff5a8d0

This is a new audit of the two current local folders only. No historical audit
or earlier port list was used as a semantic input.

Purpose
-------

Identify current KD2 implementations that are better than current KD4 and are
worth porting or adapting. Also identify areas where current KD4 is already
better, where KD2 behavior moved to a newer KD4 owner, where a KD2 idea needs a
redesign rather than a copy, and where generated/test/documentation differences
are not independent runtime ports.

No runtime source code was changed by this audit.

Coverage and closure
--------------------

Every current file below both codex-rs roots was inventoried by relative path,
size, and SHA-256, and every directory was inventoried by relative path, except
the root-level target build tree. target was excluded from content hashing but
separately enumerated and counted.

Decision rules
--------------

PORT_FROM_KD2 / direct candidate:
  A bounded KD2 implementation is better and can be brought forward with
  focused adaptation and tests.

MERGE_MANUALLY / REDESIGN:
  KD2 contains valuable behavior, but copying whole files would remove newer
  KD4 architecture or leave the feature unwired. Rebuild the behavior through
  current KD4 owners, contracts, callers, and tests.

RETAINED_ORPHANED:
  The current KD4 file exists, but current module exports or production callers
  do not reach it.

ALREADY_PRESENT / RETAINED_WIRED / MOVED:
  KD4 already owns the behavior, sometimes under a different path or newer
  abstraction. Do not port the older KD2 owner.

KD4_BETTER / SUPERSEDED / SKIP:
  Keep KD4. A KD2 copy would be a rollback, an inactive idea, a generated-only
  difference, test/support evidence, or documentation that needs rewriting.

Executive conclusion
--------------------

KD2 contains several real improvements worth preserving, especially source
discovery, command preflight and validation evidence, safer context rendering,
input validation, Windows/WSL handling, Git ownership recovery, MCP approval
compatibility, bounded tool search, local runtime proof, and a handful of
bounded TUI/process fixes.

KD4 is substantially better across code mode, plugins, skills, remote control,
newer protocol/thread models, exec-server, network and certificate handling,
auth/provider behavior, MCP/API caching, Windows sandboxing, PathUri, state and
thread-store semantics, analytics, feedback attachments, and current model UI.
Those areas must remain KD4-owned.

The safest approach is feature-slice porting. Do not copy either tree wholesale.

Supplemental P0 issue outside the KD2-vs-KD4 file comparison
-------------------------------------------------------------

Local publish omits the required code-mode sidecar.

Current evidence:
  scripts/publish-local-codex.ps1 builds/publishes codex.exe only.
  codex-rs/code-mode/src/remote_session.rs resolves
  codex-code-mode-host.exe beside the running codex.exe on Windows.
  The published LOCAL-KD directory therefore needs a manual sidecar build/copy
  before code-mode tools can start.

KD2 does not contain the fix either, so this is not a KD2 port candidate. It is
a separate KD4 publish-path defect discovered while performing the audit.

Recommended fix:
  Build and publish codex.exe plus codex-code-mode-host.exe transactionally,
  record independent hashes/backups, verify the sidecar exists beside the
  installed CLI, and exercise one code-mode startup in the publish proof.

Best bounded KD2 ports
----------------------

1. MCP approval response compatibility

KD2 owner:
  mcp-server/src/approval_response.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  One shared parser accepts both the MCP-spec response
  {action, content: {decision}} and the legacy {decision} response. Current KD4
  exec and patch approval handlers deserialize only the legacy structs, and the
  exec owner contains a TODO acknowledging the spec mismatch.

Action:
  Port the shared parser to both handlers. Preserve deny-by-default behavior and
  add accept/decline/cancel plus legacy tests for exec and patch paths.

2. Git dubious-ownership recovery

KD2 owners:
  git-utils/src/operations.rs
  git-utils/src/info.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  Read-only Git inspection retries only after the exact dubious-ownership and
  safe.directory diagnostic, using per-command
  -c safe.directory=<resolved-repository-root>. It does not mutate global Git
  configuration and has fake-Git tests for retry and unrelated failures.

Action:
  Port narrowly to inspection/stdout calls. Do not retry mutating commands and
  do not write global safe.directory configuration.

3. Context-fragment escaping and compaction

KD2 owners:
  context-fragments/src/additional_context.rs
  context-fragments/src/fragment.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 uses fixed attributed wrappers for untrusted/application context,
  XML-escapes keys and values, preserves legacy matching, and compacts repeated
  non-empty lines inside marked fragments. KD4 still forms dynamic tags from
  unescaped keys/values.

Action:
  Port the rendering behavior while retaining current KD4 protocol field names
  and updating current expectations that assert the legacy tag form.

4. Bounded, diverse tool-search caching

KD2 owner:
  core/src/tools/handlers/tool_search.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 adds normalized-query caching, bounded LRU inventories/results, candidate
  overfetch, deterministic source-diversity round-robin, trace evidence, and
  focused tests. KD4 has a simpler one-entry cache without the diversity pass.

Action:
  Adapt to KD4 StepContext and current deferred MCP/extension source identities.

5. Plan and request-user-input validation

KD2 owners:
  protocol/src/plan_tool.rs
  protocol/src/request_user_input.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 rejects empty plans, more than one in_progress step, invalid question
  counts, overlong headers, invalid option counts, and unknown fields. KD4 does
  not enforce the same bounds during deserialization.

Action:
  Port the validation while preserving current KD4 wire shapes and error style.

6. Collision-safe tool names

KD2 owner:
  protocol/src/tool_name.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 provides qualified, display-qualified, and model-callable name helpers.

Action:
  Port the helpers while keeping existing Display behavior compatible.

7. Windows command display formatting

KD2 owner:
  app-server-protocol/src/protocol/item_builders.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 recognizes Windows absolute executables and avoids POSIX shlex escaping
  that makes displayed backslash paths misleading. Execution semantics are not
  changed.

Action:
  Port the isolated formatter and focused display tests.

8. Windows app-server-test-client process handling

KD2 owners:
  app-server-test-client/src/process.rs
  app-server-test-client/src/lib.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 uses env::join_paths, env::temp_dir, netstat/taskkill on Windows, and
  lsof/kill on POSIX. KD4 test-client code still contains ':' PATH joining,
  /tmp assumptions, and POSIX-only listener cleanup.

Action:
  Port the helper and wire it into KD4's richer current test client.

9. TUI /diff untracked-file cap

KD2 owner:
  tui/src/get_git_diff.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 caps untracked file diffs at 50 and reports omitted paths. KD4 has no
  equivalent bound.

Action:
  Port with focused output/snapshot coverage.

10. Atomic/idempotent generated memory writes

Current KD4 helper:
  memories/write/src/generated_files.rs

KD2 wiring:
  memories/write/src/lib.rs
  memories/write/src/storage.rs
  memories/write/src/workspace.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD4's retained helper implementation is stronger, but it is not declared by
  lib.rs and production writers still call tokio::fs::write. KD2 wires all four
  generated-memory write paths through the helper.

Action:
  Keep the KD4 helper; port only the module/caller wiring. Validate unchanged
  content, temp cleanup, replacement, and Windows behavior.

11. Hidden Windows apply_patch stdin support

KD2 owner:
  arg0/src/lib.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 accepts stdin when the hidden --codex-run-as-apply-patch branch receives
  no argument and rejects empty, non-UTF-8, or extra input with tests. KD4's
  standalone apply_patch binary already supports stdin, but the Windows batch
  alias routes through the hidden codex.exe branch, which still rejects it.

Action:
  Share one argument-or-stdin parser with the standalone implementation and
  test the actual Windows apply_patch.bat pipeline.

12. Plain-rollout search snippets

KD2 owner:
  rollout/src/search.rs

Why KD2 is better:
  KD2 parses rg --json matches into snippets for normal rollout files. KD4 uses
  rg -l for plain files, although its compressed-rollout fallback already emits
  snippets.

Action:
  Add plain-file snippets without changing compressed search semantics.

13. Windows config-proto generator

KD2 owner:
  config/scripts/generate-proto.ps1

Why KD2 is better:
  It supplies a Windows-native Cargo-lane wrapper with line-ending and rustfmt
  normalization.

Action:
  Adapt it to current generator arguments and owner-controlled output.

14. Conditional Windows nextest headroom

KD2 owner:
  .config/nextest.toml

Why KD2 may be better:
  KD2 gives the serialized legacy Windows-sandbox lane a 30-second period with
  terminate-after=4, raising the ceiling from 60 to 120 seconds under private
  desktop/restricted-token contention.

Action:
  Add only if focused current Windows runs reproduce the pressure; it can delay
  detection of a real hang.

High-value manual integrations
------------------------------

1. Command preflight, command shape, shell summaries, verify_local, and
   validation freshness

KD2 wires command preflight, command shape, shell output summaries,
verify_local, validation evidence, unknown-mutation tracking, and final-turn
freshness through the live tool/session path. KD4 contains several matching
helper files but its current module exports, tool registration, shell/unified
exec callers, turn tracking, and final-turn path do not reach the full feature.

Rebuild this as one current-owner slice against KD4 StepContext, router,
approval, sandbox, and final-turn APIs. Isolated helper copies are insufficient.

2. Source discovery, read spans, citations, and evidence

KD2 has search_source/read_file_span handlers, source routes/search CLI,
workspace indexing, path-aware search, discovery/evidence ledgers, repo-guidance
evidence, module-map scaffolding, and source citation types. KD4 lacks the
complete end-to-end feature.

Recommended sequence:
  Port the bounded file-search source library/CLI.
  Add current protocol/config types and budgets.
  Register handlers/specs and feature visibility.
  Add evidence/session integration and parity tests.

3. Local runtime provenance and doctor proof

KD2 exports and populates build information, runtime information, capabilities,
local watermark, expected publish target, executable hash/version, repository
state, and Desktop process proof. KD4 contains several related files, but its
current protocol exports, initialize path, and doctor path do not reach them.

Merge through current app-server protocol/source owners and current CLI doctor.
Regenerate app-server schema from source and validate through an actual local
publish/restart/runtime proof.

4. App-server non-strict startup fallback

KD2 installs effective config/cloud/thread loaders after non-strict preload
failure. KD4 lacks the equivalent helper/call path.

Merge into current app-server startup while preserving strict-mode behavior.

5. Structured turn-start validation

KD2 validates ByteRange/UserInput values and emits stable invalid text range,
environment selection, and thread settings error data. KD4 lacks those pieces.

Merge protocol user_input, app-server error codes, and turn processing while
preserving KD4 image URL and current turn validation.

6. Shared coding budgets and config explain

config/src/coding_budgets.rs is byte-identical in both trees, and KD4 also has
config_explain.rs, but current KD4 does not export the modules or reconnect the
same consumers/CLI command. KD2 centralizes many limits and exposes config
explain.

Reconnect selectively. Some KD2 constants differ from current KD4 literals, so
each limit is a behavior decision, not an automatic import change.

7. WSL and client-native path handling

KD2 handles custom /etc/wsl.conf roots, Windows drive to WSL mount conversion,
mount to drive conversion, WSL UNC paths, WSL_INTEROP, and broader client path
ingress normalization. KD4 has richer PathUri/FileSystemPath types but only
partial shared/native conversion.

Keep KD4 path contracts. Add normalization at current workdir, app-server,
config, filesystem, plugin, Git, TUI, and permission boundaries. Permission and
sandbox paths require focused accepted/rejected tests.

8. Exact-snapshot patching

KD2 has exact snapshot rendering, line-ending preservation, ambiguous-anchor
and overlap rejection, a handler, schema/registration, and tests. KD4 has no
equivalent exact-snapshot tool.

Port as one PathUri-aware apply-patch safety slice. Do not replace the current
patch parser or weaken stale-read/preflight behavior.

9. Command/process outcome metadata and dynamic-tool errors

KD2 exposes duration, timeout, output-cap metadata, and an optional dynamic-tool
error. KD4 omits some outcome fields and currently computes a dynamic-tool error
into an unused variable.

Merge end to end across protocol types and producers, regenerate schema from
source, and retain KD4 remote-image and validation behavior.

10. TUI /ideas

Both folders contain the same prompt asset, but KD2 wires command metadata and
dispatch while KD4 has no current reference to the prompt.

Merge slash metadata, dispatch, and compatible exact-match popup behavior.

11. Session-quality memory, receipts, and recovery

KD2 contains dirty-worktree baseline state, execution receipts, command
classification, history continuity, repeated-tool-failure recovery, working
context briefs, Git evidence, discovery state, and cross-turn validation state.

Do not copy KD2 session owners. Re-express the useful pieces through KD4's
WorldState, StepContext, context patching, and service architecture.

12. Bounded per-turn memory retrieval

KD2 ranks memory_summary entries by relevance, scope, freshness, contradiction,
and budget, and injects a traced per-turn fragment. KD4 already injects a
bounded memory summary at thread context and supplies dedicated memory tools,
so this is an optimization/redesign rather than a missing memory system.

Adapt the selector only if measured context pressure justifies it, and replace
KD2's Debug-based user-text extraction with current typed input handling.

13. Full-repository review mode and selective auth diagnostics

KD2 has a wired full-repo review kind and useful structured auth failure
summaries. KD4 has newer review/resume plumbing.

Port the outcome and prompt kind through current review/task APIs; do not copy
the old exec or review owners wholesale.

14. Atomic, symlink-safe config persistence

KD2 centralizes symlink resolution and atomic config persistence. KD4 plugin
edits are already strong, but some marketplace/MCP edit paths still write
directly.

Adapt the helper only for weaker current owners and preserve KD4 plugin writes.

15. Additional selective ideas

Potentially useful with focused design/testing:
  Interrupted request-user-input answer preservation.
  Low-noise app-server notification suppression for successful read/list/search.
  ExecutionScope/DiscoveryDepth/MutationMode/AnswerMode only with enforcement.
  Response-item turn attribution and source/memory citation reconciliation.
  Remote-control debug-model-list persistence alias only.
  Transport-decision telemetry with a current core call site.
  Reasoning recovery audit rebuilt on current trace models with direct tests.
  Safer Execute collaboration wording: do not treat silence as scope approval,
  ask for materially risky choices, and remove hard research time ceilings.
  An updated SOURCE_MAP.md or removal of the dangling AGENTS/rules breadcrumb.

Current KD4 code that exists but is not fully reachable
-------------------------------------------------------

The following current KD4 files are present, but current exports/callers do not
activate the full behavior found in KD2:

  app-server protocol/server_info and runtime provenance/build/watermark files
  config/src/coding_budgets.rs
  config/src/config_explain.rs and tests
  core command_preflight, command_shape, verify_local, and shell summary files
  memories/write/src/generated_files.rs
  shell-command/src/rg.rs
  tui/prompt_for_ideas_command.md

Treat these as wiring/integration work, not as proof that the feature is active.

Where current KD4 is better
---------------------------

Keep KD4 and do not copy KD2 wholesale in these areas:

Code mode and host protocol:
  KD4 has the split cell actor, session runtime, process-owned remote lifecycle,
  liveness/recreation, deferred tools/MCP types, V8/JIT, and broader tests.

Core plugins:
  KD4 has marketplace policy, NPM sources, snapshots, install telemetry, typed
  source identity, remote synchronization, and richer store behavior.

Core skills:
  KD4 SkillsService, root/environment discovery, and immutable snapshots are
  stronger than KD2's manager-era design.

Remote control:
  KD4 server_api/refresh lifecycle, persistence, retry handling, and tests are
  newer. Only the small debug-model-list alias is a possible KD2 addition.

Protocol, threads, and items:
  KD4 has richer consolidated item models, PathUri contracts, thread history,
  multi-agent modes, fork lastTurnId, compacted items, current auth/capability
  types, and rollout controls.

TUI and safety:
  KD4 has richer model/reasoning popups, advertised effort handling, safety
  buffering, usage/reset flows, and newer plugin catalog/marketplace UX.

Exec-server:
  KD4 has shared lazy reconnects, fresh auth on reconnect, disconnect
  propagation, streaming backpressure, telemetry, and substantially more tests.

Network and certificates:
  KD4's centralized custom-CA HTTP/WebSocket builders are wired and tested.
  Its proxy also has credential brokerage, native certificates, locked/hashed
  managed-CA artifacts, and non-persisted private keys.

Auth, login, and providers:
  KD4 has route-aware identity, validation, fallback/cooldown, external-auth
  commit semantics, and broader provider/auth tests.

Codex API, MCP, and RMCP:
  KD4 has current file upload, safety buffering, per-user/versioned caches,
  hard refresh, OAuth locks/status, OpenAI-form elicitation, and richer
  connection management. KD4 already mirrors threadId into structured content.

Windows sandbox and PathUri:
  KD4 centralizes TTY normalization, refreshes stale credentials, expands
  restricted/elevated tests, and supports richer Windows/UNC/foreign URI rules.

State and thread store:
  KD4 adds recency, history, relation filters, newer session fields, and current
  query semantics. The targeted state/log/memory index migrations are already
  present and wired.

Rollout repair:
  KD4's empty-item reconcile path calls metadata extraction, which loads and
  applies the full rollout using current history/recency semantics. KD2's eager
  load is at most a single-read optimization, not missing functionality.

Analytics and feedback:
  KD4 retains remote-plugin/subagent behavior in newer reducers and supports
  MIME-aware binary attachments.

Moved or superseded owners
--------------------------

Examples that should not be restored at their KD2 paths:

  app-server-protocol/jsonrpc_lite.rs -> current rpc.rs
  protocol/ids/* -> current top-level session_id.rs and thread_id.rs
  split item_command.rs/item_memory.rs -> current richer consolidated item.rs
  code-mode-host codec/error ownership -> code-mode-protocol/src/host
  exec-server protocol types -> current exec-server-protocol crate
  codex-api websocket owner -> current websocket-client/current endpoints
  thread-store page-query helper -> current inline newer cursor/recency logic
  cloud retry policy -> current service ownership
  Xcode 26.4 client quirk -> current inline request-processor behavior

Explicit skips and false positives
----------------------------------

Do not port these as independent features:

  Generated app-server/config schemas, TypeScript, snapshots, Cargo.lock, or
  Bazel outputs. Regenerate through the owning workflow only after source work.

  KD2 model catalog hydration. The current KD2 models.json contains zero
  @codex-template references, so the wired hook is behaviorally inactive.

  KD2 forced gpt-5.5 default selection.

  KD2 thread FTS migration without a verified query consumer.

  KD2 low-usage/shared-editing collaboration assets as standalone files; they
  have no current registry consumer. Useful wording should go into active
  templates only.

  Broad README copying. Many KD2-only README files are breadcrumbs or empty
  placeholders. Currentize only owner/navigation documentation that is used.

  External-agent 1024-character description rejection. Current KD4 explicitly
  preserves overlong descriptions and has newer migration behavior.

  The older agent-identity mismatch guard. KD4's current API derives the runtime
  identity from a single source and removes the mismatched-input shape.

  Whole-file rollback of code mode, plugins, skills, app-server protocol, TUI,
  network, auth, MCP, sandbox, state, thread store, analytics, or feedback.

Recommended implementation order
--------------------------------

1. Fix the local publish sidecar omission so future runtime validation can use
   code mode reliably.

2. Land the smallest high-confidence ports: MCP approval compatibility, Git
   safe-directory retry, plan/request-user-input validation, context escaping,
   Windows command display/test-client helpers, /diff cap, hidden apply_patch
   stdin, and memory writer wiring.

3. Reconnect command preflight/shape/summaries, verify_local/freshness,
   coding-budget/config-explain ownership, runtime provenance/doctor, startup
   fallback, turn validation, and /ideas through current KD4 callers.

4. Add WSL/native path normalization and exec/dynamic-tool outcome contracts
   with protocol/schema and safety validation.

5. Stage source search library/CLI, source tools, citations/evidence, then
   session-quality state and recovery on WorldState/StepContext.

6. Add exact-snapshot patching and full-repository review as separate,
   safety-focused feature slices.

7. Consider optional prompt, memory-selector, notification, telemetry, and
   reasoning-audit improvements only after focused need is demonstrated.

Validation required when implementing
-------------------------------------

Rust owner changes:
  Run the smallest focused crate tests that exercise accepted and rejected
  behavior plus current caller wiring.

App-server/protocol contract changes:
  Run focused tests and just app-server-schema-check. Regenerate only through
  the owner.

Config schema changes:
  Run focused config/core tests and just config-schema-check.

Execution, permission, sandbox, and apply-patch changes:
  Run focused safety tests and prove approval/sandbox behavior is unchanged.

TUI/client-visible changes:
  Run focused unit/snapshot tests and interactive confirmation when event
  lifecycle or rendering changes.

Desktop-visible runtime proof:
  Build/publish the local CLI and sidecar, restart Codex Desktop, verify process
  path and binary hashes, and exercise the visible feature.

Audit limitations
-----------------

This was a read-only source and wiring audit. No Rust test suite or runtime
publish was needed to classify existing code, and none was run. Each accepted
port still requires implementation-specific tests and runtime proof.

The per-path appendix below accounts for every non-target file and directory in
the two current folders. Repeated rationale is intentional: every path retains
its own explicit disposition.
